reasoning: The control CCCS SI-16: Memory Protection is primarily concerned with ensuring that memory protection mechanisms are in place to prevent unauthorized access or modification. This type of control is typically more relevant to operating systems or application-level security rather than Kubernetes itself. Kubernetes does not directly manage memory protection at the node or container level; this is handled by the underlying operating system and container runtime. Therefore, there are no direct Kubernetes security controls or configurations that map to this requirement.
title: CCCS SI-16: Memory Protection
